title: Import CRDs as a KCL package
description: Generate a KCL package from Kubernetes CRDs via `kcl import -m crd`. Source the CRDs from a Crossplane provider OCI image (--image), a GitHub repo at a pinned ref (--repo), or a local directory (--from). Useful for consuming up-to-date cloud resources (e.g. provider-upjet-gcp).

name.description: Name of the KCL package to create (also the Nx project name).
name.x-prompt: Package name for the generated schemas (e.g. gcp-storage):

directory.description: Parent directory the package is created in.

from.description: Local directory (or file) of CRD YAMLs. Mutually exclusive with --repo.

image.description: Crossplane provider OCI image to extract CRDs from, e.g. ghcr.io/crossplane-contrib/provider-gcp-storage:v2.6.0. Requires docker + yq. Mutually exclusive with --from/--repo.

repo.description: Crossplane provider GitHub repo, e.g. crossplane-contrib/provider-upjet-gcp. Mutually exclusive with --from.

ref.description: Git ref/tag/branch for --repo (pin a release for reproducibility).

service.description: Comma-separated service/group prefixes to include, e.g. storage,compute. Omit to include all (large).

crdPath.description: Path to the CRD directory within --repo.

apiScope.description: Which CRD flavor to import: cluster-scoped (*.upbound.io) or namespaced v2 (*.m.upbound.io).
